# Kbd

Displays keyboard keys and shortcut sequences in instructional text.

## Installation

Add Kbd from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/kbd
```

## Usage

Wrap one key in `Kbd`, or place several keys in `KbdGroup` for a shortcut. The components describe keyboard input; they do not register, dispatch, or validate shortcuts.

```vue
<template>
  <div class="flex flex-col items-center gap-4">
    <KbdGroup>
      <Kbd>⌘</Kbd>
      <Kbd>K</Kbd>
    </KbdGroup>
    <p class="text-sm text-muted-foreground">
      Press <Kbd>Esc</Kbd> to close the command palette.
    </p>
  </div>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { Kbd, KbdGroup } from '@/Components/Kbd'
</script>
```

## Labels and platforms

Use the label users see on their keyboard, such as `Esc`, `Enter`, or an arrow symbol. Platform-specific modifiers may differ: show `⌘` on macOS and `Ctrl` where that is the real binding. If the application detects a platform, keep that logic outside Kbd and pass the resulting label into the slot.

## Composition

Kbd works inside prose, buttons, tooltips, and input adornments. Keep a visible action label when a shortcut alone would be ambiguous. A shortcut in a tooltip should reinforce an already discoverable control, not hide the control's only accessible name.

## Accessibility

The native `kbd` element conveys user input semantically, but visual key caps do not guarantee that a feature is keyboard accessible. Implement and test the real interaction separately, avoid overriding common browser or assistive-technology shortcuts, and document multi-key sequences in the order they are pressed.

## When to use

- Use Kbd in help text, command menus, and discoverable shortcut hints.
- Avoid it for decorative badges or non-keyboard tokens.
- Prefer ordinary text when the instruction is a gesture, pointer action, or voice command.

Hosted documentation: [Kbd](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/kbd)
